title: Ally
excerpt: An ally is a creature the current game state treats as aligned with another creature for targeting, protection, support, or coordinated effects.
markdown:
# Ally

An ally is a creature the current game state treats as aligned with another creature for effect resolution. Ally status matters when a spell, feature, or rule refers to protecting allies, excluding allies from harm, sharing benefits with allies, or choosing an allied target.

## Relationship State

Ally status is a relationship state, not a creature type. A creature can count as an ally because of party membership, faction alignment, a control relationship, a summoning or ownership relationship, or another explicit world-state link that places the creature on the same side for the current effect.

## Rules Use

### Targeting and Protection
- Some effects can target only an ally
- Some effects exclude allies from an area or harmful rider
- Some effects grant benefits to allies within a range or area

### Not Automatically Permanent
- Ally status can change if a creature changes sides, becomes controlled by another source, or otherwise stops being treated as aligned for the current situation
- A rule that depends on an ally should use the current relationship state at the time the effect resolves

### Separate From Attitude
- A creature can be friendly without necessarily being treated as an ally for every effect
- A creature can be neutral or temporarily cooperative without gaining allied status unless the active game state treats it as such

## Common Uses

- Spells that heal or protect allies
- Features that grant bonuses to nearby allies
- Area effects that spare allies while affecting hostile creatures
- Summoned or controlled creatures that are treated as aligned with their source
